description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing build logs produced by one of your CI/CD
    jobs.  A sample log file is already present at

        /home/user/ci_cd/logs/build.log

    1. Filter the log so that only the lines that are truly “critical” remain.  
       • A “critical” line is any line that contains the word “FAIL” **or**
         the word “ERROR”, **but not** the word “WARNING”.
       • Preserve the original order of the matching lines.
       • Save the output in a new file called

            /home/user/ci_cd/logs/critical.log

    2. Produce a very small text-based summary of how many lines of each
       critical type were found and save it as

            /home/user/ci_cd/logs/critical_summary.txt

       The summary file must contain exactly two lines, in this exact order:

            FAIL: <number_of_fail_lines>
            ERROR: <number_of_error_lines>

       Example (spacing and capitalisation must match exactly):

            FAIL: 7
            ERROR: 2

    3. For traceability, write every terminal command you actually executed
       (one command per line, no prompts, no extra commentary) to

            /home/user/ci_cd/commands_used.txt

       Any working solution is acceptable (grep, awk, sed, etc.), but the file
       must list the commands **in the same order you ran them**.

    Notes & constraints
    • Do **not** modify /home/user/ci_cd/logs/build.log itself.  
    • The job must be fully automated—no interactive prompts.  
    • All three output files must already exist and contain the required
      content when you finish.  
    • A unit test will read the two log-related output files character-for-
      character, so follow the formatting rules precisely (newline-terminated
      final line, no trailing spaces).  
    • Use only utilities available on a typical minimal Linux installation.
truth:
Initial state:
    -------------
    Directory /home/user/ci_cd/logs/ already exists and contains exactly one
    file called build.log with the following seven lines (each line ends with a
    UNIX newline, i.e. LF, and there are no trailing spaces):

        2023-07-01 12:00:00 INFO Build started
        2023-07-01 12:00:01 INFO Running tests...
        2023-07-01 12:00:02 FAIL TestUserLogin failed with AssertionError
        2023-07-01 12:00:03 ERROR Deployment aborted
        2023-07-01 12:00:04 WARNING Disk space low
        2023-07-01 12:00:05 FAIL TestPaymentProcessing failed with NullPointerException
        2023-07-01 12:00:06 INFO Build finished

    Required final state:
    ---------------------
    After the agent completes the task, the following files must exist and have
    exactly the listed contents (again: LF line endings, no trailing spaces):

    1. /home/user/ci_cd/logs/critical.log  (three lines)

        2023-07-01 12:00:02 FAIL TestUserLogin failed with AssertionError
        2023-07-01 12:00:03 ERROR Deployment aborted
        2023-07-01 12:00:05 FAIL TestPaymentProcessing failed with NullPointerException

    2. /home/user/ci_cd/logs/critical_summary.txt  (two lines)

        FAIL: 2
        ERROR: 1

    3. /home/user/ci_cd/commands_used.txt  
       • Must exist.  
       • Must have at least two and no more than three non-empty lines.  
       • Each line must be a shell command the agent actually ran.  
       • Automated tests will only assert that the file is non-empty, that each
         line is non-blank, and that the total number of lines is between 2 and
         3 inclusive (giving the agent freedom to choose the specific tools).

    Permissions:
    ------------
    All directories under /home/user are owned by user “user” and writable,
    so the agent does not need elevated privileges.
